data.narrative:
Atom Bodies is a Series A biotech & health company founded by Tyler Hayes, Doug Satzger, and Eric Monsef, with $2M+ raised to date. Valuation analysis draws on a universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms including Eikon Therapeutics, Xaira Therapeutics, Neko Health, Chai Discovery, and Asimov, alongside larger-cap peers like Abridge, Ultima Genomics, and Freenome.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression anchors to the $2M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression, implying $4M. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.3B, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.7x across three public biotech tickers) cannot be applied without target revenue disclosure.

The triangulated range spans $3M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median estimate of $652M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Atom Bodies' commercial progress, IP strength, and clinical trajectory relative to peers. Stage-progression produces a floor-like estimate materially below peer median, suggesting either substantial de-risking is required or peer valuations embed significant optionality. Revenue visibility and cash-burn trajectory are critical to narrowing this range.
